commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds purpose-specific memory role slots, legacy memory-slot migration, per-agent memory-slot overrides, and updates plugin activation, runtime, doctor, status, tests, and docs surfaces.

PR surface: Source +885, Tests +876, Docs +22, Generated 0. Total +1783 across 71 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the PR schema accepts `agents.list[].plugins.slots.contextEngine` while the new resolver only implements memory role slots. The broader PR is a feature, not a bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can remove the unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` surface; the broader memory role API still needs maintainer review before merge.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Replaces the single scalar `plugins.slots.memory` selection model with purpose-specific memory role slots so memory plugins can compose instead of displacing one another.
- Adds first-class config/schema/type/docs support for `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`, while keeping legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots` so new memory providers can be tested or scoped to one agent without changing the global memory stack.
- Wires the role-slot model through plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, config validation, doctor repair/preview surfaces, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and docs.

Why does this matter now?

- #60572 documents repeated production pressure from memory-core, LosslessClaw, Hindsight, Honcho, Mem0, ByteRover, and memory-core dreaming all solving different memory problems while competing for one global slot.
- The old slot contract forced useful tools into workarounds: context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global memory replacement.
- The branch makes the platform contract match the layered memory architecture users are already trying to operate.

What is the intended outcome?

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work and migrate/normalize toward `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` without a flag day.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, and cleanup.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This does not replace `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823; slot values remain scalar plugin IDs for this PR.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not force third-party plugins to adopt every role immediately; existing memory plugins still default to recall-compatible behavior.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Backward compatibility for legacy `plugins.slots.memory` configs and `doctor --fix` migration behavior.
- Whether role-slot-selected plugins are consistently considered active across loader, gateway startup, validation, skills, uninstall, and diagnostics.
- Per-agent override behavior, especially restrictive plugin allowlists and explicit plugin disable/deny cases.
- Preservation of context-engine behavior for LosslessClaw-style plugins.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Related #70823 for future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps the scalar slot-id model and solves the memory role composition layer first.

Related historical/runtime context gathered during analysis:

- #78540: active memory plugin diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` entries could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.
- #77063, #77185, #76576, #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior.
- #77714: persistent/pluggable memory backend demand is part of the same ecosystem pressure.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: memory plugins no longer have to globally displace each other to participate in distinct memory roles. The active memory/runtime/plugin surfaces now understand role-slot selections globally and per agent.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Dev-01, branch `feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`, using the repository Node/pnpm toolchain.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```sh
corepack pnpm exec tsx src/entry.ts config schema | jq '.properties.plugins.properties.slots.properties | keys'
corepack pnpm tsgo:core
corepack pnpm oxfmt --check ...
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...
```

Evidence after fix:

Terminal output from the real source CLI on this branch:

```text
$ corepack pnpm exec tsx src/entry.ts config schema | jq '.properties.plugins.properties.slots.properties | keys'
[
  "contextEngine",
  "memory",
  "memory.capture",
  "memory.compaction",
  "memory.dreaming",
  "memory.recall",
  "memory.userModel"
]
```

Supplemental deterministic validation:

- Focused branch batch: 6 files, 349 tests passed.
- Broader role-slot batch: 11 files, 313 tests passed.
- Branch-risk regression batch: 8 files, 378 tests passed.
- Additional focused batches: 154 tests and 62 tests passed.
- Earlier context-engine compatibility batch: 342 tests passed across plugin slot, context-engine, config, and doctor warning coverage.

Observed result after fix:

- The source CLI schema exposes `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` alongside legacy `memory` and `contextEngine`.
- `plugins.slots.memory` remains accepted as legacy recall shorthand.
- `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` canonicalizes recall selection.
- Per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots[...]` memory overrides validate and participate in plugin activation where intended.
- Role-slot-selected memory plugins are included in startup plugin IDs, loader activation, plugin skill publishing, config validation, status/doctor surfaces, and uninstall/stale-slot repair paths.
- Context-engine slot behavior remains independent; `plugins.slots.contextEngine = "lossless-claw"` is still resolved through the existing context-engine path.

What was not tested:

- A live third-party LosslessClaw install was not run from this branch.
- Future owner-provenance object semantics from #70823 are not implemented or tested here.

Proof limitations or environment constraints:

- Validation was local and focused on deterministic branch risk. Broad project gates should be covered by CI after this PR opens.
- Unit tests, typechecks, lint, and CI are supplemental to the copied CLI output above.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory slot keys, defaults, normalization, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Migrates legacy memory slot handling through doctor repair sequencing and preview warnings.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Primary risks:

- Third-party plugins that read `plugins.slots.memory` directly may need to learn the canonical `memory.recall` key for best UX, although the legacy shorthand remains supported.
- The public memory role taxonomy becomes durable API surface after merge, so naming and docs deserve careful maintainer review.
- Per-agent memory role overrides are intentionally limited to memory roles; extending other slots later should be a separate product/API decision.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm oxfmt --check ...`
- `git diff --check`
- Focused branch regression batches totaling more than 1,500 passing test cases across plugin slots, loader activation, config validation, startup plugin IDs, memory runtime, doctor repair, status scanning, plugin skills, uninstall cleanup, dreaming, context-engine compatibility, and related config surfaces.

CI should run the full project gate for final merge confidence.
